source.title: Selected practice recommendations for contraceptive use, fourth edition
source.population: People seeking initiation, continuation, switching, or support for contraceptive method use after medical eligibility is established.
source.exclusions: Global exact guidance for informed contraceptive choice and practical method use; partial for UAE-specific law, prescribing, and full medical-eligibility assessment, which requires the companion MEC and local review. This batch also reviews method-follow-up, entered effects, and emergency-contraception sections. Eligibility, product selection, dosing, procedures and UAE availability remain clinician responsibilities. exact_location.heading: 1.6 Quality of care and access to products

source.evidence_paraphrase: Specifies method information on effectiveness, correct use, common effects, risks and benefits, return-to-clinic signs, fertility, STI protection, and opportunities for questions.

items.final_wording: Supports concurrent female and male evaluation, duration trying to conceive, menstrual, obstetric, gynaecologic, medical, surgical, medication, lifestyle, sexual, STI, partner, and prior-investigation history.

source.title: Guideline for the prevention, diagnosis and treatment of infertility
source.population: Individuals and couples being evaluated and managed for infertility.
source.setting: Primary, reproductive-health, fertility, and specialist services adaptable to national settings.
source.exclusions: Exact global guidance for infertility history, focused examination, diagnostic evaluation, and person-centred support; requires UAE adaptation and does not directly cover recurrent pregnancy loss as a separate presentation.

exact_location.heading: Chapter 3.1–3.3 — objective, indication, initial consultation, and medical history

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports concurrent female and male evaluation, duration trying to conceive, menstrual, obstetric, gynaecologic, medical, surgical, medication, lifestyle, sexual, STI, partner, and prior-investigation history.

items.rationale: Supports fertility history only when seeking pregnancy is entered.

items.final_wording: Requires preference-sensitive discussion, psychosocial support, transparent information, and consent-based collaborative decisions.

source.evidence_paraphrase: Requires preference-sensitive discussion, psychosocial support, transparent information, and consent-based collaborative decisions.
